name: Norman Mailer

acts.description: Stabbed his second wife Adele Morales with a penknife at a party in 1960; she nearly died
Persistent misogynistic ideology expressed in major essays and public life

behavior_summary: Stabbed his wife at a party, avoided serious charges after she declined to press them, went on to win the Pulitzer Prize. Wrote extensively about the virtues of male violence.

art.summary: Two-time Pulitzer Prize winner. A central figure in American letters for fifty years. Pioneered New Journalism alongside Tom Wolfe.

accountability_events: 1960 charges dropped after wife declined to press

verdict.blurb: He stabbed his wife at a party and continued to be Norman Mailer, winner of two Pulitzer Prizes and iconic American literary figure. He wrote extensively about male violence as vitality. This was not entirely theoretical.
